title: mcp-companion project configuration
description: Per-project visibility overrides for MCP servers, read by mcp-companion.nvim from .mcp-companion.json walked up from the editor's current working directory at chat-session creation time. The file selects which servers (declared in your servers.json / mcpServers config) are exposed to a session in this project, overriding the global cc.auto_http_tools / cc.auto_acp_tools / cc.auto_cli_tools settings. Overrides are keyed by adapter / agent name (not by interaction kind), so one file applies to HTTP chats, ACP chats, and CLI sessions alike.

allowed_servers.description: Names of MCP servers that should be visible to chat sessions in this project. All other configured servers are hidden. Mutually exclusive with disabled_servers.

disabled_servers.description: Names of MCP servers that should be hidden from chat sessions in this project. All other configured servers remain visible. Mutually exclusive with allowed_servers.

tool_system_prompts.description: Whether to add per-tool natural-language system messages alongside the tools array. Useful for models that ignore JSON-Schema descriptions; set to false to save tokens. Overrides the plugin-level cc.tool_system_prompts setting; defaults to true.

auto_approve.description: Per-server auto-approve overrides for in-process CodeCompanion tool calls in this project. Keys are server names (proxied servers from your servers.json, or native servers such as 'neovim'). Each value is the auto-approve spec and overrides the plugin-level setting for that server in this project. Applies to in-process CodeCompanion chats only; external ACP/CLI agents use their host harness's own approval.

additionalProperties.description: Auto-approve spec: true (approve all tools from this server), false or [] (prompt for all), or an array of match tokens — tool-name globs (e.g. 'read_*', 'get_*', 'open_file') and/or 'tier:<read|navigate|write|exec>' aliases. Tier aliases match only native tools (proxied tools carry no tier). A tool is approved if any token matches.

adapters.description: Per-adapter server filter overrides. Keys are adapter names for CC chats (e.g. 'moonshot-ai', 'claude', 'copilot_acp') or agent names for CLI sessions (e.g. 'claude_code'). Each value overrides the top-level allowed_servers / disabled_servers for sessions using that specific adapter / agent, allowing different models to see different subsets of MCP servers within the same project.

allowed_servers.description: Names of MCP servers visible to this adapter in this project. All other configured servers are hidden. Mutually exclusive with disabled_servers.

disabled_servers.description: Names of MCP servers hidden from this adapter in this project. All other configured servers remain visible. Mutually exclusive with allowed_servers.
